items.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
items.authors: Lizhi Yang, Junheng Li, Nehar Poddar et al.
items.abstract: For a humanoid robot to be deployed in the real world, the choice of command space (i.e., the interface between task planning and whole-body control) is crucial. Existing whole-body controllers typically demand dense kinematic or spatial references that planners struggle to synthesize from task sema...

items.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
items.authors: Liliana Hotsko, Yinxi Li, Yuntian Deng et al.
items.abstract: Code language models need repository-level context to resolve imports, APIs, and project conventions. Existing methods inject this knowledge as long inputs (retrieved through RAG or dependency analysis) or through per-repository fine-tuning and LoRA -- costly at repository scale and brittle to evolv...

items.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
items.authors: Sondos Mahmoud Bsharat, Jiacheng Liu, Xiaohan Zhao et al.
items.abstract: As AI writing assistants become increasingly integrated into real-world drafting and revision workflows, many documents are no longer purely human-written or AI-generated, but instead result from progressive human-AI co-editing. However, existing AI-text detection benchmarks largely focus on final o...

items.title: PAR3D: A Unified 3D-MLLM with Part-Aware Representation for Scene Understanding
items.authors: Shaohui Dai, Yansong Qu, You Shen et al.
items.abstract: Recent advances in 3D multimodal large language models (3D-MLLMs) have enabled unified solutions for 3D scene understanding tasks, including visual question answering, captioning, and referring segmentation. However, existing 3D-MLLMs remain largely object-centric, limiting their ability to model fi...

items.title: Complexity-Balanced Diffusion Splitting
items.authors: Noam Issachar, Dani Lischinski, Raanan Fattal
items.abstract: Standard continuous-time generative models rely on monolithic architectures that must navigate vastly different signal regimes, from isotropic noise to intricate data distributions. While scaling model capacity improves performance, deploying a massive network uniformly across the entire generative ...

items.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
items.authors: Marius Dragoi, Ioana Pintilie, Alexandra Dragomir et al.
items.abstract: Parameter-efficient finetuning methods based on spectral decomposition have enabled progress in Continual Learning. In this paper we introduce TailLoR, which utilizes the singular bases U and V of the pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame to learn a low-rank update applied to the singular v...

items.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
items.authors: Dong Jing, Jingchen Nie, Tianqi Zhang et al.
items.abstract: Robot manipulation alternates between low-risk transit phases that call for fast execution and high-risk contact stages that demand slow, precise motion. Yet existing Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) only inherit a single fixed speed from training demonstrations. Prior efforts to accelerate VLAs...
